text: Knowledge Distillation (Hinton, Vinyals, Dean 2015, Google) transfers knowledge from a large teacher model to a smaller student by training on soft targets (probability distributions) rather than hard labels.

The Lottery Ticket Hypothesis (Frankle & Carbin 2019, MIT) showed that dense, randomly-initialized networks contain sparse subnetworks that can be trained in isolation to match the full network's accuracy.

Deep Compression (Han et al. 2016, Stanford/NVIDIA) combined pruning, trained quantization, and Huffman coding to reduce model size by 35-49× without accuracy loss, establishing the compression pipeline used in TinyML.
